key_learnings: Identified key gene-nutrient interactions involving MTHFR, APOE, and FTO variants; learned how single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) affect folate metabolism, fat processing, and obesity risk; established that personalized nutrition based on genotype outperforms generalized dietary guidelines.
research_findings: APOE4 carriers show heightened sensitivity to saturated fat intake and elevated LDL response; MTHFR C677T variants benefit from increased folate and B12 supplementation; FTO gene variants are associated with reduced satiety signaling, influencing overeating tendencies and weight management strategies.
current_focus_areas: Research on nutrient metabolomics to identify biomarkers for diet response; expanding the gene panel to include variants related to vitamin D metabolism and caffeine sensitivity; developing algorithms to generate genotype-based meal plans from sequencing data.
